Residential Rental Agreement (Sample Document)
This Rental Agreement is made between the Landlord and the Tenant. This document is a simple
example contract that can be used to test document uploading, processing, and AI question
answering in the Legal AI Platform.
1. Parties
The Landlord agrees to rent the property located at 10 Example Street, and the Tenant agrees to
pay rent under the terms defined in this agreement.
2. Rent Payment
The Tenant agrees to pay a monthly rent of 1200 USD on the first day of each month.
3. Late Payment
If the rent is not paid within 5 days of the due date, a late fee of 50 USD may be applied.
4. Security Deposit
The Tenant shall pay a refundable security deposit equal to one month of rent.
5. Maintenance

a late fee of 50 USD may be applied.
4. Security Deposit
The Tenant shall pay a refundable security deposit equal to one month of rent.
5. Maintenance
The Tenant agrees to maintain the property in good condition and notify the Landlord of any
damages or repairs required.
6. Termination
Either party may terminate the agreement with a 30-day written notice.

agreement
signed
on

that
some

Sami Krichen, external counsel for Atlas Build - Mr. Youssef Ben Salem, warehouse supervisor at Nour Trading ================================================== 2. [REDACTED_ID] [REDACTED_ID] [REDACTED_ID] ================================================== On 10 January 2026, Atlas Build SARL and Nour Trading SARL signed a Supply Agreement
for
the
delivery
of
cement,
steel
bars,
insulation
panels,
and
waterproofing
materials.

las Build SARL and Nour Trading SARL signed a Supply Agreement
for
the
delivery
of
cement,
steel
bars,

panels,
and

materials.
Relevant contractual terms: 2.1 Payment Terms Payment shall be made within thirty (30) calendar days from the date of invoice. All invoices not paid by the due date shall accrue a late penalty of 1.5% per month.
Any undisputed invoice remaining unpaid for more than fifteen (15) days after written notice
may
constitute
a
material
breach.
2.2 Delivery Terms Atlas Build shall deliver goods to Nour Trading's main warehouse in Sfax within seven (7)
business
days
of
each
confirmed
order.
Time shall not be of the essence unless expressly stated in writing for a specific order. Delivery delays caused by transport strikes, border closures, force majeure events, or
customs
issues
shall
not
automatically
constitute
breach.

rder. Delivery delays caused by transport strikes, border closures, force majeure events, or
customs
issues
shall
not
automatically
constitute
breach.
2.3 Inspection and Defect Notification Nour Trading shall inspect delivered goods within five (5) business days of receipt. Any alleged non-conformity or defect must be notified in writing, with reasonable supporting
evidence,
within
that
inspection
period.
Failure to notify within the required period shall be deemed acceptance of the goods, except
in
case
of
hidden
defects.
2.4 Termination Clause Either party may terminate this Agreement in the event of a material breach by the other
party,
provided
that:

hidden
defects.
2.4 Termination Clause Either party may terminate this Agreement in the event of a material breach by the other
party,
provided
that:
- the non-breaching party gives written notice of the breach, - the breaching party is granted a cure period of ten (10) business days, - the breach remains uncured after the cure period expires. 2.5 Dispute Resolution Any dispute arising from this Agreement shall first be subject to an amicable settlement
attempt
for
fifteen
(15)
calendar
days
after
written
notice
of
dispute.
If no settlement is reached, the dispute shall be submitted to the competent courts of Tunis. 2.6 Governing Law This Agreement is governed by Tunisian commercial law. 2.7 Confidentiality The parties agree to keep pricing schedules, supplier lists, discount arrangements, and
non-public
commercial
terms
confidential.

2.7 Confidentiality The parties agree to keep pricing schedules, supplier lists, discount arrangements, and
non-public
commercial
terms
confidential.

- Atlas Build logistics sheet lists delivery on 20 January 2026 for the first order. - Nour Trading's warehouse log mentions receipt on 22 January 2026. - Similar discrepancies exist for the second and third orders. 3. There is evidentiary risk because the file currently lacks: - signed delivery slips for Order AB-NT-002, - email acknowledgment of receipt for the 12 March notice from Nour Trading, - photographs or technical inspection reports disproving the alleged defects, - proof that "time was of the essence" for any of the disputed deliveries. 4. rather

globally.

regional

substantial

================================================== 7. [REDACTED_ID] LEGAL RISKS ================================================== The following legal risks are identified in this file: - Risk of disputed delivery dates weakening a clean summary judgment claim. - Risk that termination is declared invalid if exercised before the cure period expires. - Risk of procedural objection if litigation begins before completion of the amicable
settlement
period.

if exercised before the cure period expires. - Risk of procedural objection if litigation begins before completion of the amicable
settlement
period.
- Risk that Atlas Build cannot fully rebut defect allegations due to missing technical reports. - Risk that Nour Trading's set-off argument may complicate immediate recovery. - Risk from missing signed delivery slips for at least one order. - Risk of incomplete proof of receipt of the formal breach notice. - Risk of commercial relationship breakdown and reputational harm. - Risk of inconsistency between invoice records and warehouse logs. - Risk that some claims for late penalties are overstated if not calculated invoice by invoice. ==================================================

gs. - Risk that some claims for late penalties are overstated if not calculated invoice by invoice. ==================================================
8. [REDACTED_ID] [REDACTED_ID] / NEXT [REDACTED_ID] ================================================== The legal team recommends collecting the following items urgently: - Signed delivery slips for Orders AB-NT-001, AB-NT-002, and AB-NT-003. - Warehouse entry logs from Nour Trading for all three deliveries. - Transport company proof of handover and delivery timestamps. - Photos or expert inspection results regarding the allegedly damaged insulation panels. - Email server logs proving delivery of the notice sent on 12 March 2026. - Courier proof of receipt or refusal for the formal notice. - Accounting statement showing unpaid status of invoices as of 18 March 2026. - Any resale contract or downstream loss document relied on by Nour Trading. - Internal communication confirming whether urgent delivery deadlines were ever agreed in

tract or downstream loss document relied on by Nour Trading. - Internal communication confirming whether urgent delivery deadlines were ever agreed in
writing.

writing.
Suggested next legal actions: - Recalculate accrued late-payment penalties as of 20 March 2026. - Send a reminder letter immediately after the cure period expires if no payment is received. - Avoid filing suit before the amicable settlement period expires. - Prepare a draft statement of claim for possible filing in Tunis after 27 March 2026. - Consider without-prejudice settlement contact on 24 March 2026. ================================================== 9. KEY DATES FOR [REDACTED_ID] [REDACTED_ID] [REDACTED_ID] ================================================== 10 January 2026 - Supply Agreement signed 14 February 2026 - Due date for invoice INV-[REDACTED_PHONE] 06 March 2026 - Due date for invoice INV-[REDACTED_PHONE] 12 March 2026 - Formal breach notice sent 13 March 2026 - Earliest assumed notice receipt date 17 March 2026 - Response from Nour Trading 21 March 2026 - Due date for invoice INV-[REDACTED_PHONE] 24 March 2026 - Suggested settlement contact date 26 March 2026 - Estimated end of 10 business day cure period 27 March 2026 - Estimated end of amicable settlement period 28 March 2026 onward - possible timing for court filing, subject to final procedural verification ================================================== 10. [REDACTED_ID] EMAIL DRAFT INPUT [REDACTED_ID] ================================================== Plain-language client update points: - Atlas Build has a strong contractual basis to request payment of at least 79,500 TND for

====================== Plain-language client update points: - Atlas Build has a strong contractual basis to request payment of at least 79,500 TND for
the
first
two
invoices.
- Nour Trading is contesting payment by alleging late delivery and defects.
- Some of those allegations are currently unsupported by detailed evidence. - Atlas Build should be careful not to terminate too early. - Atlas Build should also avoid going to court before the amicable settlement period ends. - The file still needs stronger delivery and notice evidence. - A settlement attempt may still be commercially useful before litigation. ================================================== 11. SHORT [REDACTED_ID] ================================================== This case is suitable for testing: - case summarization - deadline extraction - legal risk detection - grounded question answering - client email drafting - source citation behavior End of test document.

Agreement ID: MSA-AR-NL-2026-004 Effective Date: January 12, 2026 Parties: - Atlas Retail Group SARL ("Atlas"), represented by CEO Karim Ben Salem - Nova Logistics Tunisia SARL ("Nova"), represented by Director Lina Mzoughi ## 1. Scope of Services Nova will provide inbound warehousing, order packing, and national last-mile

delivery

services

for

Atlas

ecommerce

orders. ## 2. Service Levels (SLA) - On-time delivery target: 96% monthly. - Lost package maximum threshold: 0.8% monthly. - Escalation response time: 4 business hours. - Breach trigger: if SLA target is missed for two consecutive months, Atlas

may

issue

a

formal

notice

of

non-compliance. ## 3. Commercial Terms - Monthly fixed operations fee: 21,500 TND. - Variable delivery fee: 8.70 TND per delivered order. - Fuel surcharge cap: 4.5%. - Payment terms: Net 30 from invoice date. - Late payment interest: 1.5% per month after due date. ## 4. Invoicing and Audit - Invoice issue date: no later than the 5th day of each month. - Atlas may audit supporting delivery logs with 5 business days prior notice. - Disputed invoice lines must be notified within 10 calendar days of receipt. ## 5. Data Protection and Confidentiality - Customer personal data may only be processed for order fulfillment. - Data retention for delivery proof records: 24 months. - Security incident reporting deadline: within 24 hours of discovery. ## 6. Term and Termination - Initial term: 12 months from Effective Date. - Auto-renewal: 12 months unless either party gives 30 days written notice

before

renewal

date. - Immediate termination permitted in case of material breach not cured within

- Auto-renewal: 12 months unless either party gives 30 days written notice

before

renewal

date. - Immediate termination permitted in case of material breach not cured within

15

days

after

written

notice.

## 7. Liability - Service credits apply if on-time delivery drops below 94%. - Liability cap: direct damages capped at three months of average fees. - Liability cap does not apply to fraud, gross negligence, or intentional

misconduct. ## 8. Dispute Resolution - Governing law: Tunisia. - Step 1: executive negotiation within 10 business days. - Step 2: mediation in Tunis. - Step 3: if unresolved, arbitration under agreed commercial arbitration

law: Tunisia. - Step 1: executive negotiation within 10 business days. - Step 2: mediation in Tunis. - Step 3: if unresolved, arbitration under agreed commercial arbitration

rules. ## 9. Operational Timeline - January 12, 2026: Agreement signed. - February 1, 2026: Go-live date. - February 5, 2026: First invoice due to be issued. - March 7, 2026: Atlas sent first SLA warning email. - March 18, 2026: Nova acknowledged temporary routing disruption. ## 10. Signatures Signed in Tunis on January 12, 2026 by authorized representatives of both

parties.

of

rights. ## 1. SLA Non-Compliance Atlas records indicate: - February 2026 on-time delivery: 92.4% - March 2026 on-time delivery: 93.1% - Lost package ratio in March: 1.2% These metrics are below contract thresholds and trigger corrective action

Atlas notes line-item anomalies in: - Fuel surcharge adjustments - Returned parcel handling fees - Weekend delivery uplift entries ## 3. of

variable

volumes

and

and

impact.

if

plan

is

notice

of

non-compliance. ## 3. Invoicing and Audit - Invoice issue date: no later than the 5th day of each month. - Atlas may audit supporting delivery logs with 5 business days prior notice. - Disputed invoice lines must be notified within 10 calendar days of receipt. ## 5. Data Protection and Confidentiality - Customer personal data may only be processed for order fulfillment. - Data retention for delivery proof records: 24 months. - Security incident reporting deadline: within 24 hours of discovery. ## 6. Term and Termination - Initial term: 12 months from Effective Date. - Auto-renewal: 12 months unless either party gives 30 days written notice

# Agent Test Runbook (Fast) ## 1) Create one case Example title: `Atlas vs Nova Logistics - SLA Dispute` ## 2) Upload docs (same case) 1. `01_master_service_agreement_pdf_ready.md` (exported as PDF) 2. `02_notice_of_breach_and_payment_dispute_pdf_ready.md` (exported as PDF)

3. `03_counterparty_response_pdf_ready.md` (exported as PDF) ## 3) Upload voice - Record yourself reading `04_voice_intake_script_for_transcription.txt` or upload an audio file of

it. - Wait for transcription status `completed`. - Click `Create intake request`. ## 4) Copilot prompts to test each agent - `Summarize case #<CASE_ID>` - `List deadlines for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Analyze risks for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Build timeline for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Review booking for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Compare documents in case #<CASE_ID>` - `Draft client email for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Optimize prompt: draft a negotiation email for case #<CASE_ID>` - `What external legal trends might affect warehouse SLA disputes in Tunisia

for

email for case #<CASE_ID>` - `Optimize prompt: draft a negotiation email for case #<CASE_ID>` - `What external legal trends might affect warehouse SLA disputes in Tunisia

for

case

#<CASE_ID>` ## 5) What success looks like - Sources appear in Evidence tab. - At least one answer includes external web references (when external research

is

enabled). - Workflow tab produces stage traces and verified summary. - Intake tab shows extracted client + urgency + schedule from transcript. - Intelligence tab shows type, entities, summary, and redacted preview.
